Now I have a clear picture of all project files. Let me wait for the msbuild-code-review agent to finish its analysis, then I'll synthesize a comprehensive report.

The issues I can already see:

1. **Duplicated properties across ALL 5 projects**: TargetFramework, LangVersion, Nullable, ImplicitUsings, TreatWarningsAsErrors, EnforceCodeStyleInBuild, Company, Authors — all identical across all 5 projects.

2. **No Directory.Build.props** — these common properties should be centralized.

3. **No Directory.Build.targets** — the `PrintBuildInfo` target is duplicated in all 3 src projects.
   
4. **No Directory.Packages.props** — package versions are scattered and inconsistent across projects, with `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` varying between 8.0.0 and 8.0.1, and test packages like `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`, `xunit`, and `xunit.runner.visualstudio` differing significantly between test projects.

5. **DocumentationFile** is also duplicated.

6. **IsPackable=false** appears in both test projects as well. I should wait for the agent to finish gathering the full picture before putting together a complete report.

data.content:
The review agent is still running. Let me synthesize findings from what I've already read — the issues are clear from the project files.

Here's a comprehensive analysis of your build infrastructure:

---

## 🔍 MSBuild Review: ContosoApp Solution

### Issue 1: Massive Property Duplication (All 5 projects)

Every project repeats these **7 identical properties**:
```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

**Fix:** Create a `Directory.Build.props` at the repo root:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

---

### Issue 2: Inconsistent Package Versions

| Package | Api | Core | Services |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` | **8.0.0** | **8.0.0** | **8.0.1** ⚠️ |

| Package | Api.Tests | Core.Tests |
|---|---|---|
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | **17.9.0** | **17.10.0** ⚠️ |
| `xunit` | **2.7.1** | **2.8.1** ⚠️ |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | **2.5.6** | **2.8.1** ⚠️ |

**Fix:** Enable NuGet Central Package Management with a `Directory.Packages.props`:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Src packages -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <!-- Test packages -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Then remove `Version="..."` from all `<PackageReference>` items in `.csproj` files.

---

### Issue 3: Duplicated `PrintBuildInfo` Target

The same custom target is copy-pasted across all 3 `src/` projects. 

**Fix:** Create a `Directory.Build.targets` at the repo root:

```xml
<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

Then remove the `<Target>` blocks from each `.csproj`.

---

### Issue 4: Duplicated `DocumentationFile` in src projects

All 3 source projects set `<DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>`.

**Fix:** Add to `Directory.Build.props` conditionally for non-test projects:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

---

### Issue 5: Duplicated Test Project Boilerplate

Both test projects repeat `<IsPackable>false</IsPackable>` and the same set of test framework packages.

**Fix:** Add to `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
</PropertyGroup>
```

And create a `test/Directory.Build.props` to auto-include shared test packages:

```xml
<Project>
  <Import Project="$([MSBuild]::GetPathOfFileAbove('Directory.Build.props', '$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..'))" />
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" />
    <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

---

### Summary: What each `.csproj` becomes after cleanup

**`ContosoApp.Api.csproj`** (from 33 → 10 lines):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
    <ProjectReference Include="..\ContosoApp.Services\ContosoApp.Services.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

**`ContosoApp.Core.csproj`** (from 27 → 7 lines):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

**Test projects** (from 26 → 6 lines each):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

---

### Files to create

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Directory.Build.props` | Common properties for all projects |
| `Directory.Build.targets` | Shared `PrintBuildInfo` target |

## 🔴 Errors (2)

### E1: Inconsistent `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` version across projects

| Project | Version |
|---|---|
| `ContosoApp.Api` | **8.0.0** |
| `ContosoApp.Core` | **8.0.0** |
| `ContosoApp.Services` | **8.0.1** ⚠️ |

Services references `8.0.1` while Api and Core reference `8.0.0`. Since Api → Services → Core form a dependency chain, NuGet will resolve this to the highest version at build time, but the intent is unclear and this creates a maintenance hazard. If a breaking behavioral change slips in, it's invisible. This **will** trigger `NU1605` package downgrade warnings in certain restore scenarios.

### E2: Inconsistent test infrastructure versions between test projects

| Package | Api.Tests | Core.Tests |
|---|---|---|
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | **17.9.0** | **17.10.0** |
| `xunit` | **2.7.1** | **2.8.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | **2.5.6** | **2.8.1** |
| `NSubstitute` | 5.1.0 | 5.1.0 |

Two test projects running different xunit major-minor versions can lead to different test discovery behavior, different assertion APIs, and confusing failures that only reproduce in one project. This is a direct consequence of having no centralized version management.

---

## 🟡 Warnings (5)

### W1: 8 properties copy-pasted across ALL 5 project files — no `Directory.Build.props`

Every single project file repeats this identical block:

```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

That's **8 properties × 5 files = 40 duplicated lines**. When you need to move to `net9.0`, you'll need to edit 5 files. When a new team member adds a 6th project, they may forget `TreatWarningsAsErrors` or use a different `LangVersion`.

**Fix — create `/Directory.Build.props`:**

```xml
<Project>

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- All test projects are non-packable -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>
```

Then **remove** those 8 properties from every `.csproj`. Each project file should only contain what's unique to it.

### W2: No Central Package Management — package versions scattered across 5 files

There are **10 distinct package references** across the solution with versions hardcoded in each `.csproj`. This directly caused E1 and E2 above.

**Fix — create `/Directory.Packages.props`:**

```xml
<Project>

  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Shared dependencies -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />

    <!-- Test dependencies -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

Then change all `<PackageReference>` entries in `.csproj` files to **remove the `Version` attribute**:

```xml
<!-- BEFORE -->
<PackageReference Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />

<!-- AFTER (version comes from Directory.Packages.props) -->
<PackageReference Include="xunit" />
```

### W3: Custom target `PrintBuildInfo` duplicated in 3 source projects

The identical target appears in `Api.csproj`, `Core.csproj`, and `Services.csproj`:

```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

This also has a **correctness concern**: the target lacks `Inputs`/`Outputs`, so it will always execute and never be skipped during incremental builds. For a `<Message>` task this is harmless but sets a bad precedent.

**Fix — create `/Directory.Build.targets`:**

```xml
<Project>

  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>

</Project>
```

Then **remove** the `<Target>` block from all three `.csproj` files.

### W4: `DocumentationFile` duplicated in 3 source projects with a restated default path

All three source projects have:

```xml
<DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
```

The path `$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml` is exactly what the SDK generates automatically when you use the modern property. Also, test projects don't generate docs — this is an asymmetry that should be explicit.

**Fix — in `Directory.Build.props`, add for source projects:**

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Then **remove** the `<DocumentationFile>` PropertyGroup from all three source `.csproj` files. The SDK auto-generates the correct path.

### W5: Missing `PrivateAssets="all"` on `xunit.runner.visualstudio`

In both test projects:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="..." />
```

This is a test-infrastructure package that should not flow to downstream consumers. While test projects are typically leaf nodes (nothing references them), it's still best practice.

**Fix:**

```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" PrivateAssets="all" />
```
